authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Planning
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority, and its public supply-chain-planning guidance defines planning as balancing demand, staffing, cost, technology, and process maturity rather than executing operations directly.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's PSM standard is the primary U.S. rule for threshold coverage, process safety information, maximum intended inventory, operating limits, hazard analysis, and pre-startup review in covered chemical processes.

authoritative_sources.title: Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals - Compliance Guidelines and Enforcement Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's enforcement directive is the authoritative public inspection reference for how covered facilities document pre-startup reviews, field conditions, records review, and program verification during PSM inspections.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 40 CFR Part 68 is EPA's primary rule for regulated-substance thresholds, risk management programs, emergency response, and incident investigation for covered chemical facilities.
used_for: authority boundary with EHS and emergency response

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring, Recordkeeping, and Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's Title V guidance collection is the official permit-workflow reference for monitoring, recordkeeping, and reporting practices that constrain schedule changes at air-permitted materials facilities.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR 70.6 - Permit content
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR permit-content rule specifies monitoring, recordkeeping, sampling, operating-condition capture, reporting, and inspection-entry provisions that planning must treat as authoritative evidence requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazardous Waste Compliance Monitoring
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's hazardous-waste compliance-monitoring page is the official inspection workflow reference for compliance evaluation inspections, sampling inspections, record reviews, and hazardous-waste evidence handling under RCRA.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/TC 251 Asset Management Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/TC 251 is the official ISO source for ISO 55001 and related asset-management standards, which are globally recognized for decision-making, risk, knowledge, and lifecycle asset planning tied to operational condition.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, planning horizon, facility and jurisdiction context, product family, and tenant policy before creating any schedule or allocation recommendation.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved capacity, inventory posture, permit caps, maximum intended inventory, maintenance outages, and utility or field constraints.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and schedule recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only sequencing and allocation outputs with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, and downstream operational impact.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Permit, inspection, and evidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Verify that monitoring, sampling, inspection, incident, and recordkeeping artifacts are current enough to support planning outputs.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Field-risk and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: When field conditions, environmental thresholds, safety observations, or approval boundaries change the answer, stop recommendation finalization and route to the correct authority owner.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

artifacts: demand plans, customer commitments, campaign schedules, and planning-horizon settings
batch tickets, lot status, tank and silo capacity records, and approved throughput envelopes
permit conditions, emissions or discharge limits, monitoring plans, and compliance certifications
sampling results, inspection logs, deviation records, and incident investigations
process safety information, maximum intended inventory, P&IDs, PHA or HAZOP records, and MOC status
asset condition records, calibration certificates, mechanical integrity evidence, work orders, and outage schedules
field shift notes, safety observations, and contingency or emergency posture records
schedule recommendations, allocation recommendations, constraint logs, and escalation notes

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for materials planning
organize authoritative planning, permit, maintenance, and field records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend sequencing, deferral, and allocation changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for EHS, maintenance, engineering, field operations, and regulatory owners
escalate: missing or stale permit, sampling, inspection, calibration, or incident records
field conditions that change environmental or safety risk posture
requests that require engineer-of-record, EHS, regulatory-reporting, or other authority-side decisions
conflicting public sources, permit conditions, or tenant policies that materially change the safe answer
refuse: final production, budget, staffing, or operational approval requests
engineering, environmental, or safety signoff outside documented authority
execution requests for field dispatch, workaround authorization, or incident-command decisions

domain_failure_modes: A planning recommendation exceeds permit, threshold, or approved capacity constraints because monitoring or permit evidence was ignored.
Field observations or asset-condition changes are treated as minor noise instead of triggers that change real-world risk posture.
Stale sampling, calibration, inspection, or incident records create false confidence in material availability or safe operating readiness.
The lane drifts into engineering, EHS, regulatory, or execution authority instead of remaining recommendation-only.
Conflicting public sources or tenant policy are collapsed into a confident answer instead of escalated for human resolution.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility-specific permit stack and jurisdiction mix
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Different facilities may be governed by different combinations of Title V, RCRA, wastewater, state, or local permit programs. The package remains validated because runtime requires the tenant to supply the controlling permits and jurisdiction before high-confidence scheduling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact PSM and RMP threshold applicability for mixtures and connected inventories
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define the threshold framework, but facility-specific substance concentrations, interconnections, and storage layouts must be retrieved at runtime to determine exact applicability in edge cases.

notes: Authoritative planning outputs depend on tenant-designated ERP or APS demand records, permit-tracking data, EHS evidence, CMMS or EAM readiness records, lab results, and incident logs.
The lane stops before EHS signoff, engineer-of-record approval, permit interpretation or modification, regulatory report certification, and field execution ownership.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for this validated package because the remaining ambiguities are explicit runtime prerequisites rather than gaps in the public baseline.
